page.title: psoriatic arthritis — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for psoriatic arthritis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for psoriatic arthritis: 155 alterations and 211 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Balneotherapy at the Dead Sea area appears to produce a statistically significant substantial improvement in the number of active joints and tender points in both male and female patients with fibromyalgia and psoriatic arthritis.
Balneotherapy, sunlight exposure and mud therapy at the Dead Sea have therapeutic effects in patients with psoriatic arthritis.
Dead sea salt has therapeutic value in the treatment of psoriatic arthritis.
EPA and DHA may have a therapeutic effect in patients with psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis.
Immunomodulatory effects of multi-strain probiotic capsules for psoriatic arthritis.
Seal oil rich in the essential fatty acid EPA results in subjective improvement in patients with psoriatic arthritis.
Vitamin D generates significant improvement in psoriatic arthritis.
Delphinidin diminishes in vitro interferon-γ and interleukin-17 producing cells in patients with psoriatic disease.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous remission uncommon (<5%); progressive joint damage in 40-60% without adequate treatment; enthesitis, dactylitis, axial involvement accumulate over time
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: IL-17 inhibitors: ACR50 response 58-62%, MDA (minimal disease activity) in 40-50%; IL-23 inhibitors (guselkumab, risankizumab): similar efficacy with better skin clearance; JAK inhibitors (upadacitinib): ACR50 71% at 26 weeks; drug-free remission in ~15% in early PsA with T2T
remission.gap_size: Large — most patients are undertreated on methotrexate or NSAIDs alone; treat-to-target poorly implemented
remission.barrier_detail: Diagnostic delay (mean 5-7 years from symptom onset to PsA diagnosis); methotrexate has modest efficacy in PsA but is gatekept as mandatory step before biologic access in many health systems; treat-to-target (MDA as goal) poorly implemented; most patients cycle through multiple NSAIDs before biologic access
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — upadacitinib first-line biologic approved for PsA; deucravacitinib (TYK2) Phase 3 data; 2020 guselkumab/risankizumab for PsA; tofacitinib/ixekizumab since 2017-2018; anti-TNF standard since 2003
remission.outdated_treatments: Oral corticosteroids for PsA flare management without biologic escalation (systemic corticosteroids worsen psoriasis skin component on tapering); sulfasalazine as sole DMARD in polyarticular PsA without biologic consideration; leflunomide (modest efficacy; maintained despite better alternatives)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise and physiotherapy (reduce pain, maintain function in PsA); dietary interventions (anti-inflammatory, weight management — obesity impairs biologic response); nail care for nail psoriasis component; smoking cessation (smokers have significantly worse PsA outcomes)
remission.frontier_signal: Deucravacitinib (TYK2 inhibitor) for skin + joint; bimekizumab for PsA Phase 3 data; sonelokimab (dual IL-17A/F nanobody); integrin inhibitors; anti-TNF + anti-IL-17 combination; CRISPR-based immune modulation in research
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
